name: Monitors
description: Persistent watchers that check for conditions on a schedule or every agent pulse. A monitor describes WHAT to look for, WHY it matters, and WHAT TO DO when the condition triggers. Monitors emit Alerts when triggered.

properties.description: What the monitor watches. Examples: 'Login frequency for sarah@meridian.com', 'HubSpot sync freshness', 'New high-intent replies', 'account_score for tier-A accounts'.
Human-readable description: WHY this matters. Example: 'Alert if drops below 3 logins/week — early churn signal'.

How the monitor evaluates. threshold = value crosses a number; change_detection = value moves materially; schedule = run on cadence; absence = expected event didn't happen; anomaly = AI-detected outlier.
How often the monitor runs. every_pulse = each agent cycle; on_event = triggered by webhook or signal arrival.

What happens when the condition triggers. notify = create an Alert. escalate = create an Alert + create a high-priority Task. auto_act = run a specific operation. log_only = append to workspace updates.
When action='auto_act', the operation name to run (e.g. 'analyze.reply-sentiment', 'act.notify-rep-handoff').
Whether the monitor is running. Paused monitors don't evaluate; retired monitors are kept for history.
What the monitor is scoped to. 'global' = org-wide; 'email' = specific contact; etc.
Value of the linked entity's key. Use memory_filter_by_property(type='monitor', custom_key_value=...) to find all monitors for an entity.
Project / campaign this monitor belongs to, when applicable.

JSON-encoded condition expression evaluated by the monitor runtime. Shape varies by Type — examples: { property: 'login_frequency', operator: 'lt', value: 3 } for threshold; { property: 'last_sync_at', operator: 'older_than', value: '24h' } for absence.
Lifetime count of times this monitor has triggered. Useful for tuning noisy monitors.
